$comment: D-CSUBSET-VLA C4b: a VLA TYPEDEF (`typedef int R[n]; R a;`) RUNS on all 3 legs. C99 §6.7.7p2 evaluates the size expr ONCE at the typedef and FREEZES it; every later `R a;` allocates with that frozen size. Two witnesses carry the weight: (A) FREEZE-ONCE — after mutating `n` to 100 between two `R` objects, BOTH `a` and `b` stay int[3] (proven by the runtime `sizeof a == sizeof b == 12`; a leak would re-eval n=100 => 400), returns 1/2 the strict catch; (B) MULTI-DIM OFF-DIAGONAL — `c[1][0]` vs `c[0][1]` (and `d`'s) are DISTINCT cells, so a wrong copied-down runtime row stride would alias/transpose them (returns 5/6/9/10). `sizeof c[0]` / `sizeof d[0]` pin the copied-down per-level row slots. main is a LEAF (no calls) — the VLA frame-model scope. The arm64 leg proves the copy-down stride math on the non-host target; the `release` arm proves R's frozen size slots + the copied-down slots survive the full optimizer (never folded to a constant, never dropped). exitCode 42; return 1..12 are the strict in-program pins.
